fields.indication_summary: Selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor (SSRI) indicated for the treatment of major depressive disorder (MDD), obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD), panic disorder (PD), posttraumatic stress disorder (PTSD), social anxiety disorder (SAD), and premenstrual dysphoric disorder (PMDD).
fields.dosage_forms_strengths: Tablets: 25 mg, 50 mg and 100 mg; Oral solution: 20 mg/mL

notes: Two NDA numbers cover the two formulations combined into one label (NDA019839 tablets, NDA020990 oral solution), same multi-NDA pattern as XELJANZ. All three revision dates agree on 8/2023 (PI, Medication Guide 'Revised: 8/2023', SPL footer) -- no cross-date ambiguity. Same labeler as the LIPITOR document in this set (Viatris Specialty LLC), useful as a pair for labeler-extraction tests. Multiple package NDCs across strengths and the oral solution (58151-574-93, 58151-575-93/-88, 58151-576-93/-88, 58151-601-35); no single value can represent that, so ndc left null. The oral solution has a formulation-specific contraindication (disulfiram, due to alcohol content) that does not apply to tablets -- a subtle formulation-conditional fact worth testing extraction against.
